meta.title: Opportunity Class Placement Test — Thinking Skills — Practice Test 3
meta.notes: Questions Q4, Q10, Q16, Q22, Q28 have null answers due to visual/spatial content that cannot be confidently interpreted from image analysis.

questions.question: Inge and Finn first met on a Tuesday. They met again 8 days later. They then met for the third time 4 days later. On which day did Inge and Finn meet for the third time?
questions.reasoning: First meeting: Tuesday. 8 days later: Tuesday + 8 days = Wednesday (the following week, since 7 days = same day, 8 = next day). Then 4 days after Wednesday: Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday. Third meeting is Sunday.

questions.question: Cecilia does not like apple or orange juice. She will visit the stand as long as she can buy a drink she likes. Juice availability: lemon (Mon, Wed), orange (Tue, Wed, Thu), apple (Thu, Fri), grape (Mon, Tue, Fri). On which day will Cecilia not visit the juice stand?
questions.reasoning: Cecilia likes lemon and grape only. Monday: lemon + grape available. Tuesday: grape available. Wednesday: lemon available. Thursday: only orange and apple — she likes neither. Friday: grape available. Thursday is the only day with no juice she likes.

questions.question: Nick wants to learn Wiradjuri. Medika suggests spending time in Wagga Wagga where people speak Wiradjuri. Which statement best supports Medika's claim?
options: Nick can travel to Wagga Wagga fairly easily.
Learning a language always takes hard work and concentration.
Medika has learned to speak three different languages.
Languages are easier to learn if you practise speaking them with other people.
questions.reasoning: Medika's claim is that being around Wiradjuri speakers in Wagga Wagga will help Nick learn. D directly supports this by stating languages are easier to learn through practice with other speakers.

questions.question: The wooden puzzle fits together to make a cube. Pairs of blocks will fit to make one of the three layers. How are blocks 1–6 paired?
questions.reasoning: This requires analysing 3D block shapes to determine which pairs combine to form complete rectangular layers. The shapes are complex irregular pieces that must interlock. Cannot be confidently determined from image analysis alone.

questions.question: Simon says a news report claiming a third of Australians swim regularly can't be true because he and his friends rarely swim. Which sentence shows Simon's mistake?
options: Simon may prefer other forms of exercise, such as running.
Swimming may have become more popular in recent years.
The news report that Simon is watching may be incorrect.
Simon may live in an area where there are few opportunities to go swimming.
questions.reasoning: Simon uses his personal experience to deny a national statistic. D explains that his local area may have few swimming options, so his personal sample is not representative of all Australians. This shows his mistake of generalising from a small, potentially biased sample.

questions.question: Sugar glider structure has three levels for food, water bowl, and hollow log. Food is lower than water bowl, log is higher than food. Which statement must be true?
options: The middle level has the log.
The lowest level has the food.
The highest level has the water.
The middle level has the food.
questions.reasoning: Food < water (food lower than water bowl). Food < log (log higher than food). Both water and log are above food. If food were on the middle level, both water and log would need to be on the top level — impossible since there's only one top level. Therefore food must be on the lowest level.

questions.question: John needs red and green robots. His friend will swap a spare green robot for a pink one. Which pack should John buy?
questions.reasoning: John needs: a red robot (direct) and a pink robot (to swap for green). Pack 1: red, blue, yellow — no pink. Pack 2: red, yellow, pink — has both red AND pink. Pack 3: green, pink, purple — no red. Pack 4: blue, orange, pink — no red. Only pack 2 provides both red and pink.

questions.question: Melanie says pollution must be causing fish decline because the lake became more polluted. Which sentence shows her mistake?
options: The number of fish in other lakes might not have decreased over the past year.
Pollution may also cause health problems in humans.
The number of plants in the lake might not have decreased over the past year.
More people may have fished from the lake over the past year.
questions.reasoning: Melanie assumes pollution is the only possible cause because it correlates with the fish decline. D provides an alternative explanation — increased fishing could also explain the decrease. Just because pollution increased doesn't mean it's the sole cause.

questions.question: Students grow plants: geranium (canteen), guppy plant (music room), wax plant (school hall), bromeliad (library). Wax plant bloomed before guppy but after bromeliad. Geranium bloomed same day as wax plant. Which bloomed a day before any others?
questions.reasoning: Bloom order: bromeliad first, then wax plant (after bromeliad), geranium same day as wax plant, then guppy plant last. The bromeliad bloomed before all others.

questions.question: 16 shapes in Set 2 should be identical to shapes in Set 1. How many shapes in Set 2 are not identical to a shape in Set 1?
questions.reasoning: This requires comparing each of the 16 shapes in Set 2 against all 16 shapes in Set 1 to find non-matching ones. The shapes are small geometric forms that may be rotated or reflected. Cannot be confidently determined from image analysis alone.

questions.question: A gardener says talking to plants is a good idea because you'll remember to water them if you treat them like friends. Which statement best supports the gardener's claim?
options: People often notice details about a plant's health when they are talking to it.
Some people believe that plants can understand what people say to them.
Talking to plants won't help people avoid overwatering them.
Talking to plants is a very old tradition.
questions.reasoning: The gardener's claim is that talking to plants is a good idea. A provides additional support: talking to plants helps you notice health details, which is another benefit of the practice beyond just remembering to water them. B is about plant understanding (irrelevant), C weakens the claim, D is about tradition (not a reason it's good).

questions.question: Four friends in a car (2 front, 2 back). Katelyn is either driving or beside Talia. Talia is behind the driver. Jimmy is not in the back and not beside Andrea. Who is driving?
questions.reasoning: Talia is behind the driver → Talia is in the back. Jimmy is not in the back → Jimmy is in the front. Katelyn is either driving or beside Talia (in the back). If Katelyn is beside Talia, both Jimmy and Andrea are in front. But Jimmy is not beside Andrea — contradiction since both front seats are adjacent. So Katelyn must be driving. Jimmy is in the front passenger seat. Talia and Andrea are in the back.

questions.question: Letter values (whole numbers ≥ 1): TOO=4, TWO=6, HOOT=6, THAT=7. What is the score for THAW?
Iluka is in the queue at Sliced Bread Sangers (15 min). He thinks going to Loafing Around (<5 min walk, 10 min wait) would be faster. Which sentence shows his mistake?
options: Sliced Bread Sangers may make better sandwiches than Loafing Around.
Sliced Bread Sangers may have a wider range of sandwiches than Loafing Around.
The sandwiches at Loafing Around may be more expensive than those at Sliced Bread Sangers.
The queue at Loafing Around may be longer than the one at Sliced Bread Sangers.
questions.reasoning: Iluka compares only making time (15 vs 10 min + walk), but ignores that Loafing Around may also have a queue. If their queue is long enough, the total time there could exceed the remaining time at Sliced Bread. D identifies this overlooked factor in his time calculation.

questions.question: Only children not going to the observatory are those playing in the soccer match. Maliyan says Mei doesn't play soccer so she's going. Robyn says Kirra isn't going so she must play soccer. Whose reasoning is correct?
questions.reasoning: The rule: not going → plays soccer (contrapositive: doesn't play soccer → going). Maliyan: Mei doesn't play soccer → going. This is the valid contrapositive. Correct. Robyn: Kirra not going → plays soccer. This directly follows the stated rule. Correct. Both use valid logic.

questions.question: Four friends sit at a square table with four wooden blocks (sphere, cuboid, cylinder, cone). Given a top-down layout and a specific side view showing a triangle and rectangle, which friend has that view?
questions.reasoning: This requires determining which friend's perspective matches the given side view (triangle on left, rectangle on right) based on the top-down arrangement of blocks. The 3D-to-2D perspective mapping depends on exact block positions relative to each viewer. Cannot be confidently determined from image analysis alone.

questions.question: Grant Valley High School scored zero goals all season. Which must also be true?
options: Grant Valley High School came bottom of the competition.
Grant Valley High School lost more matches than any of the other teams.
Grant Valley High School won fewer matches than any of the other teams.
Grant Valley High School failed to win any matches.
questions.reasoning: To win, you must score more goals than the opponent. If they scored 0 goals total, they can never outscore any opponent. They could draw 0-0 (getting draw points) but never win. So they definitely failed to win any matches. They didn't necessarily come bottom (could draw) or lose the most matches.

questions.question: Jacob won 3/14 games, Nicole won 11/12 games. Tahnee says Nicole's higher fraction means she's the better player. Which shows Tahnee's mistake?
options: There may be another player in the class who is better than Nicole.
Jacob might have played against better opponents in his games.
Nicole has not played as many games as Jacob.
They may have played lots of other games outside of the chess club.
questions.reasoning: Tahnee compares win fractions but ignores opponent quality. B points out Jacob may have faced much stronger opponents, which would explain his lower win rate without meaning he's weaker. Opponent difficulty makes raw fractions an unreliable comparison.

questions.question: Magician school timetable (Mon–Thu, 3 slots each). Wand needed for: spells, charms, herbs. Wednesday cancelled. How many wand classes this week?
questions.reasoning: Timetable: Mon (stars&sky, farsight, spells), Tue (potions&poisons, herbs, charms), Wed (spells, broom flight, farsight — CANCELLED), Thu (herbs, stars&sky, broom flight). Wand classes remaining: Mon=spells(1), Tue=herbs+charms(2), Thu=herbs(1). Total=4.

questions.question: Sandwich ingredients: lettuce, mayonnaise, cheese, chicken. Rules: lettuce must not touch mayo, cheese must not touch lettuce, mayo must go directly on top of chicken. Which ingredient is on top?
questions.reasoning: Mayo directly on top of chicken forms a fixed block: ...chicken, mayo... Lettuce can't touch mayo, so lettuce can't be directly above mayo. Cheese can't touch lettuce. Try bottom-to-top: lettuce, chicken, mayo, cheese. Lettuce doesn't touch mayo ✓, cheese doesn't touch lettuce ✓, mayo on top of chicken ✓. Top ingredient is cheese.

questions.question: A vet says careful handling of pet baby animals (kittens, puppies) is important for their development. Which statement best supports this?
options: Some people have pets at home that were once wild animals.
Handling puppies and kittens often helps them to be less afraid of people.
Many people help wild animals by putting out food and water for them.
Handling puppies and kittens is something many people enjoy doing.
questions.reasoning: The vet's claim is that handling pet baby animals is important for proper development. B directly supports this by providing a concrete developmental benefit: it helps them be less afraid of people, which is part of proper socialisation/development.

questions.question: A wall is decorated with identical tiles (some rotated). A 2×2 section is shown. Which option shows the tile design?
questions.reasoning: This requires analysing a complex black-and-white tile pattern and determining which single tile design, when rotated in four orientations, produces the shown 2×2 wall section. Cannot be confidently determined from image analysis alone.

questions.question: Peter meets Zhang at 09:00. Sam meets Tim at 10:00. Tim meets Ursula at 11:00. Ursula meets Fiona at 12:00. Which additional meeting enables Fiona to receive the book today?
questions.reasoning: Peter gives book to Zhang at 9:00. Need a chain from Zhang to Fiona. C: Zhang meets Tim at 10:30 → Tim meets Ursula at 11:00 → Ursula meets Fiona at 12:00. Chain: Peter→Zhang(9)→Tim(10:30)→Ursula(11)→Fiona(12). All times are forward. A fails (Sam doesn't have the book at 9:30). B fails (no way to get book to Tim). D fails (Ursula-Fiona meeting is at 12:00, before Zhang-Ursula at 12:30).

questions.question: Mr Murray says warm weather must be the only reason his crocuses didn't grow, ruling out possums. Which shows his mistake?
options: The crocuses may have been affected both by warm weather and by possums.
The warmer winter may have allowed possums to find other food sources.
Possums may also like to eat other flowering plants such as tulips.
Possums may only dig up crocus plants to eat if they cannot find anything else.
questions.reasoning: Mr Murray identifies one cause (warm weather) and dismisses the other (possums). But A points out both causes could have contributed simultaneously. Finding evidence for one cause doesn't rule out the other — multiple factors can operate together.

questions.question: Four diagrams show object pairs at the same scale. Car=1.5m, person=2m, swing=2.5m, farm shed=3m. Which is tallest: dinosaur, house, tree, or truck?
questions.reasoning: From the diagrams: dinosaur ≈ 3× car = 4.5m; house ≈ 3× person = 6m; tree ≈ 3× swing = 7.5m; truck ≈ 1× farm shed = 3m. The tree, at approximately 7.5m, is clearly the tallest. Even with estimation uncertainty, the tree's ratio to its reference (swing=2.5m) is the largest, and the truck barely matches its reference (shed=3m).

questions.question: A teacher says fixing leaking taps is the most effective way to reduce water waste at home. Which statement best supports this?
options: A huge amount of water is wasted in people's homes every year.
It is very easy to fix taps that are leaking.
Leaking taps are the biggest source of water waste in households.
There are many different ways to reduce water waste at home.
questions.reasoning: The teacher claims fixing taps is the MOST effective method. C directly supports this by stating leaking taps are the biggest source of waste — fixing the biggest source would naturally be the most effective reduction method.

questions.question: Boxes W, X, Y, Z with different treasure amounts. Clue 1: X>Z. Clue 2: W>Y. Clue 3: W>X. Which could be the fourth clue to fully determine the order?
options: Box Y contains more treasure than box X.
Box X contains more treasure than box Y.
Box W contains more treasure than box Z.
Box Y contains more treasure than box Z.
questions.reasoning: From clues 1-3: W>X>Z and W>Y. Missing: relationship of Y to X and Z. A (Y>X): gives W>Y>X>Z — fully determined. B (X>Y): gives W>X>Z and X>Y, but Y vs Z unknown — two possible orderings. C (W>Z): already known from W>X>Z — adds nothing. D (Y>Z): gives W>X>Z and W>Y>Z, but X vs Y unknown. Only A uniquely determines the order.

questions.question: Unusual dice: opposing faces sum to 5, one face has 0 dots, all faces different. Which diagram shows a possible view?
questions.reasoning: The six faces must be 0,1,2,3,4,5 with opposing pairs (0,5),(1,4),(2,3). Three visible faces sharing a vertex cannot include an opposing pair. Determining which option satisfies these constraints requires precise reading of dot counts on the dice images. Cannot be confidently determined from image analysis alone.

questions.question: Ad says: anyone who loves musicals will love Carimba! Tina says Connor adores Carimba so he must love musicals. Which shows Tina's mistake?
options: Carimba! may have won the award for best musical.
Carimba! may appeal to people who do not normally like musicals.
Carimba! may be Connor's favourite movie.
Carimba! may have changed the kind of things Connor likes to watch.
questions.reasoning: The ad says: loves musicals → loves Carimba. Tina reverses this: loves Carimba → loves musicals. This is the converse fallacy. B directly challenges this by noting Carimba may appeal to non-musical fans — Connor could love Carimba without loving musicals in general.

questions.question: Tennis: 5 matches each, need ≥4 wins to qualify. Darren and Monaro both won first 3, about to play each other. Alyssa says at least one will qualify. Emily says they can't both qualify. Whose reasoning is correct?
questions.reasoning: After their match (match 4), the winner has 4 wins from 4 matches. Even if they lose match 5, they have 4/5 wins — qualifies regardless. So at least one qualifies. Alyssa is correct. The loser has 3 wins, 1 loss after 4 matches. If they win match 5: 4/5 = qualifies. So it's possible both qualify. Emily is wrong — both CAN qualify if the loser wins their 5th match.
